symptoms.turf: Silver dollar-sized bleached spots, sunken lesions
symptoms.leaf: Hourglass-shaped lesions with tan centers and reddish-brown margins
symptoms.morning: White cottony mycelium visible in dew

cultural_control.nitrogen: Maintain adequate N (0.1-0.2 lb N/1000/week during pressure)
cultural_control.irrigation: Water early morning, avoid evening irrigation

chemical_control.resistance_concern: Common resistance to DMIs and SDHIs

symptoms.turf: Circular patches 6 inches to several feet, smoke ring at edge
symptoms.leaf: Irregular tan lesions with dark borders
symptoms.morning: Gray-purple 'smoke ring' visible in morning dew

cultural_control.nitrogen: Reduce N during high-risk periods (<0.25 lb N/1000)
cultural_control.irrigation: Water early morning only, deep and infrequent

chemical_control.preventive_timing: Begin when night temps reach 60F for 2-3 nights

pythium_blight.pathogen_type: Oomycete (water mold) - not a true fungus

symptoms.turf: Greasy, water-soaked patches that turn brown, streaked pattern

pythium_blight.spread_pattern: Can follow drainage, spread rapidly overnight

chemical_control.note: Standard fungicides do NOT work - need Oomycete-specific products

symptoms.turf: Circular patches with green centers (frog-eye), bronze to straw color
symptoms.root: Dark, rotted roots with black strands (runner hyphae)

environmental_triggers.stress: Heat and drought stress trigger symptoms

summer_patch.timing: Root infection in spring, symptoms appear in summer

cultural_control.pH: Acidify to 6.0 with sulfur or ammonium sulfate
cultural_control.irrigation: Deep, infrequent to encourage deep roots

chemical_control.timing: Apply when soil temp reaches 65F (before symptoms)
chemical_control.application: Water in immediately to reach roots

symptoms.foliar: Irregular yellow patches, black acervuli on leaves
symptoms.basal: Rotted crowns and stolons, plants pull easily

environmental_triggers.stress: Any stress - heat, drought, low N, compaction, low mowing
environmental_triggers.temperature: Symptoms appear in summer heat stress

anthracnose.key_concept: Weak pathogen - only attacks stressed turf

chemical_control.note: Cultural practices are more important than fungicides

symptoms.turf: Bronze to brown patches, often with Poa annua centers
symptoms.root: Black, rotted roots with dark runner hyphae

environmental_triggers.manganese: Low Mn availability at high pH
environmental_triggers.new_establishment: More severe in newly established areas

chemical_control.timing: Apply in fall and spring preventively

symptoms.turf: Rapid blighting and thinning during warm, humid weather
symptoms.leaf: Gray to tan lesions that can girdle leaves

environmental_triggers.temperature: Warm weather, especially summer stress periods
environmental_triggers.moisture: High humidity and extended leaf wetness
environmental_triggers.nitrogen: Excessive soluble nitrogen can increase risk

cultural_control.nitrogen: Avoid excessive quick-release N during pressure
cultural_control.mowing: Avoid mowing highly stressed wet turf when possible

symptoms.turf: Dark green rings, mushrooms, or hydrophobic/dead arcs
symptoms.soil: Water-repellent thatch or soil can be present

environmental_triggers.thatch: Organic matter and thatch can support activity

cultural_control.wetting_agents: Use wetting agents when hydrophobicity is present
cultural_control.cultivation: Core/aerify or needle tine to improve water movement
cultural_control.irrigation: Water in materials when the label requires soil movement

symptoms.leaf: Small purple to brown lesions that may expand and coalesce

environmental_triggers.stress: Low mowing, drought stress, and traffic can worsen injury

cultural_control.mowing: Avoid unnecessary low mowing during stress

symptoms.turf: Pink to tan patches during cool, wet weather
symptoms.morning: Pink mycelium may be visible under active conditions

environmental_triggers.moisture: Persistent wet leaf surface and poor drying

cultural_control.surface_moisture: Improve drying and reduce prolonged leaf wetness

symptoms.turf: Circular straw-colored patches after snow cover
symptoms.morning: Gray-white mycelium may be visible as snow melts

environmental_triggers.snow_cover: Extended snow cover over unfrozen or wet turf
environmental_triggers.canopy: Long, lush turf going into winter increases risk

cultural_control.fall_nitrogen: Avoid excessive late fall soluble N
cultural_control.debris: Remove leaves and debris before snow cover
cultural_control.mowing: Do not leave turf excessively tall entering winter

pythium_root_rot.pathogen_type: Oomycete (water mold) - not a true fungus

symptoms.turf: Decline, thinning, and poor stress tolerance
symptoms.root: Poor root mass and discolored roots under wet conditions

environmental_triggers.stress: Heat, traffic, or low oxygen rootzone stress

cultural_control.drainage: Improve drainage and oxygen in the rootzone
cultural_control.cultivation: Use cultivation to relieve compaction when appropriate

chemical_control.note: Use oomycete-specific products; ordinary fungicide programs may not cover Pythium.

symptoms.turf: Circular dead patches visible as bermudagrass breaks dormancy
symptoms.roots: Dark, weakened roots and stolons may be present

environmental_triggers.season: Fall infection with symptoms visible in spring
environmental_triggers.stress: Low potassium, compaction, and winter stress can worsen symptoms

cultural_control.nutrition: Maintain balanced fertility and adequate potassium
cultural_control.timing: Plan preventive work in fall rather than waiting for spring symptoms

symptoms.turf: Ring or frog-eye patches, often on Kentucky bluegrass
symptoms.root: Root decline and dark runner hyphae may be present

environmental_triggers.stress: Root stress, compaction, and summer heat reveal symptoms

cultural_control.irrigation: Deep, infrequent watering to support roots
cultural_control.overseeding: Use tolerant species or cultivars where practical

symptoms.turf: Yellow to tan rings or patches during cool weather

cultural_control.surface_moisture: Reduce leaf wetness and improve air movement

symptoms.surface: Green to black slick surface film or crust
symptoms.turf: Thinning where surface drainage and air movement are poor

environmental_triggers.drainage: Poor drainage, shade, and low air movement

cultural_control.air_movement: Improve drying with air movement and traffic management
cultural_control.cultivation: Open the surface when sealed or crusted
